blocks.text: followed his eyeline. "Whatever you're doing over there — stop it."
had been watching him watch. She said nothing. She'd seen him go quiet like this before.
looked at the fire. He'd been doing enough of it and he knew Mara knew.
appeared at his elbow. She had a specific look she got when she'd been patient long enough.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You are sitting here watching that woman like she is a case file open on your desk and I can see it from ten feet away. We have been out here for two hours.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: She deflected six questions in four minutes. Completely — not evasions, real answers. But every one was exactly the minimum length needed to close the question. You don't get that clean without having done it before.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: She is on a trip with her friends and she had a long drive and some people are private. That's allowed.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: Private people give short answers. They don't consistently calibrate the exact right length. There's a difference.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Julian. I have worked with you for three years. You cannot take a bonfire and run behavioral profiles on it.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: You are doing the thing where your face goes very still and your eyes stop moving. You are absolutely running something.
She was right. I'd been doing it since Nadia said the fire was getting low.

blocks.speaker: Mara Voss
blocks.content: Yes. We are on vacation and you were actually here for parts of tonight and those were the good parts, Julian. Look at the water. Look at the stars. Let the rest of it go.

blocks.speaker: Julian Crane
blocks.content: And I'm looking at the water.

blocks.prompt: He's looking at the water. Is he actually here?

options.label: Actually, yes. The water is good and he lets it be good.
He looks at the water. The pattern is already filed. He just stops talking about it.
He looks at the water. And then at her looking at the water. That's not the same as nothing.
